sourceMovie.title: The Hunger Games
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Hunger Games (2012) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Hunger Games clicked for you, The Hunger Games: Catching Fire (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Hunger Games clicked for you, The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Hunger Games?
faq.answer: Try The Hunger Games: Catching Fire, The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 1, Divergent—each shares DNA with The Hunger Games while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Hunger Games?
faq.answer: The Hunger Games (2012) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Hunger Games part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Hunger Games Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
